DTR  |  Listen to a Short Exchange
LSE-HS10-L4-1400P-HSELA910-MTR-AAD-02 • HS English Grades 9–10 • Delineating and Evaluating Argument

The Sheriff's Own Words

Click   🎧   to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a girl and a boy. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once.
Listen to the Conversation
QUESTION:
Why does the girl tell the boy to change his opening?
Choose the best answer. You may change your answer before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Construct a complete academic justification. State your claim about why the girl redirects the boy's opening, support that claim with reasoning drawn directly from what she says, and articulate the conclusion that follows about the kind of evidence she requires.
CA ELD PI.B.5  |  CCSS RI.9-10.8  |  HS English Grades 9–10: Delineating and Evaluating Argument
